assembly_instructions: Open Source Ecology Compressed-Earth-Block press. Four adjustable threaded legs support a welded base plate. A rectangular cage of four vertical 3×3 inch tube posts and two upper side rails forms the chassis. A horizontal hydraulic cylinder mounted at the rear pushes a polished ram rod forward; a hardened pressing plate at the end of the rod drives into a steel mold chamber in the middle of the frame. Soil drops into the mold through an open-bottom hopper atop the mold, fed in turn from a 3'×6' tractor-loading grate with internal deflector plates; a side-mounted shaker vibrates the hopper so soil flows evenly. On the forward end of the mold, a discharge chute catches the finished 4×6×12 inch block. A glass-fronted control panel sits on the operator side and an external hydraulic-power-unit connector on the rear feeds the cylinder at 2,400 psi.

info.japanese_name: CEBプレス（通称リベレーター）
info.english_name: Open Source Ecology CEB Press — 'The Liberator'
info.summary: Open-source, fully automated hydraulic press that compresses moist soil into stabilized earth blocks for low-cost construction. Part of OSE's Global Village Construction Set (GVCS).
info.description: The CEB (Compressed Earth Block) Press, nicknamed 'The Liberator', is an open-source earth-block press developed by Open Source Ecology (OSE) and its contributor community as part of the 50-machine Global Village Construction Set. A tractor-fed hopper dumps moist, stabilized soil onto a 3'×6' grating with internal deflector plates; a hopper shaker vibrates the soil column so it flows evenly into the steel mold below. A horizontal hydraulic cylinder at the rear of the welded-tube chassis drives a polished chrome ram rod forward through the mold at ≈2,400 psi, compressing the soil against the mold walls into a dense 4×6×12 inch block. The ram retracts, the mold opens, and the finished block is pushed out the forward discharge chute while the hopper refills the mold for the next cycle. All control and sequencing is handled by an on-board panel that actuates solenoid valves on the hydraulic manifold; hydraulic pressure is supplied externally by an OSE PowerCube or a LifeTrac tractor's hydraulic take-off. Machine weight is ≈1,700 lb; it fits in the bed of a 3/4-ton pickup. Full CAD, wiring, and build documentation is published under CC BY-SA 4.0.
info.operator: Open Source Ecology + independent builders and housing co-ops worldwide
info.contractor: Open Source Ecology (Marcin Jakubowski et al.)
info.contract_date: First publicly documented build 2011 · iterated through v17.08
info.status: Open-source, production-proven; dozens of independent builds on record

facts.label: Architecture
facts.value: Horizontal-ram hydraulic press with vertical soil feed

facts.label: Footprint
facts.value: Fits in a 3/4-ton pickup bed; adjustable legs for uneven ground

facts.label: Soil input
facts.value: 3' × 6' grate with internal deflector plates for tractor loading

facts.label: Hydraulic supply
facts.value: External — OSE PowerCube or LifeTrac PTO

sources.title: Open Source Ecology Wiki — CEB Press

parts.name: Adjustable Leg (Fwd-Left)
parts.role: Threaded adjustable foot at the forward-left corner — levels the press on uneven ground.

parts.name: Adjustable Leg (Fwd-Right)
parts.role: Threaded adjustable foot (forward-right).

parts.name: Adjustable Leg (Rear-Left)
parts.role: Threaded adjustable foot (rear-left).

parts.name: Adjustable Leg (Rear-Right)
parts.role: Threaded adjustable foot (rear-right).

parts.name: Welded Base Plate
parts.role: Steel base plate / lower frame the legs bolt into and every subassembly mounts onto.

parts.name: Vertical Post (Fwd-Left)
parts.role: 3×3 inch square-tube post at the forward-left corner of the chassis cage.

parts.name: Vertical Post (Fwd-Right)
parts.role: Forward-right post.

parts.name: Vertical Post (Rear-Left)
parts.role: Rear-left post.

parts.name: Vertical Post (Rear-Right)
parts.role: Rear-right post.

parts.name: Upper Frame Rail (Port)
parts.role: Top port-side rail tying the forward and rear posts together.

parts.name: Upper Frame Rail (Starboard)
parts.role: Top starboard-side rail.

parts.name: Horizontal Hydraulic Cylinder
parts.role: Double-acting hydraulic cylinder mounted longitudinally at the rear of the chassis. Extends the ram rod forward to compress soil at ≈2,400 psi; retracts to reset.

parts.name: Ram Piston Rod
parts.role: Chrome-finished piston rod that emerges from the hydraulic cylinder and pushes the pressing head into the mold.

parts.name: Pressing Head
parts.role: Hardened platen bolted to the ram rod. Its face forms one end of the finished block as it slides through the mold.

parts.name: Brick Mold Chamber
parts.role: Hardened steel mold with an opening in the top (for soil from the hopper), an opening on the ram side (for compression), and an opening on the forward side (for ejection).

parts.name: Soil Hopper
parts.role: Tapered open-bottom hopper sitting directly on the mold opening. Meters soil into the mold between compression cycles.

parts.name: Internal Soil Deflector
parts.role: Inclined deflector plate inside the hopper funneling tractor-dumped soil toward the mold opening.

parts.name: Soil Input Grate (3' × 6')
parts.role: Coarse 3' × 6' grating on top of the hopper. A tractor bucket dumps soil onto the grate; the grate screens out rocks and breaks up soil clumps.

parts.name: Hopper Shaker
parts.role: Eccentric-cam hopper vibrator clamped to the port-side hopper wall. Keeps the soil column flowing.

parts.name: Block Discharge Chute
parts.role: Forward discharge chute — the finished block slides out here as the ram cycles.

parts.name: Compressed Earth Block (sample)
parts.role: A finished 4 × 6 × 12 in compressed earth block resting on the discharge chute.

parts.name: Control Panel
parts.role: Operator control panel — starts/stops the automated press cycle and drives the solenoid valves on the hydraulic manifold.

parts.name: Hydraulic Power-Unit Connector
parts.role: Quick-disconnect hydraulic manifold with supply/return ports for an external PowerCube or a LifeTrac tractor's auxiliary hydraulics.
